verses.1: For the music director; according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph. My voice rises to God, and I will cry aloud; My voice rises to God, and He will listen to me.
verses.2: In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord; In the night my hand was stretched out and did not grow weary; My soul refused to be comforted.
verses.3: When I remember God, then I am restless; When I sigh, then my spirit feels weak. Selah
verses.4: You have held my eyelids open; I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
verses.5: I have considered the days of old, The years of long ago.
verses.6: I will remember my song in the night; I will meditate with my heart, And my spirit ponders:
verses.7: Will the Lord reject forever? And will He never be favorable again?
verses.8: Has His favor ceased forever? Has His promise come to an end forever?
verses.9: Has God forgotten to be gracious, Or has He in anger withdrawn His compassion? Selah
verses.10: Then I said, “It is my grief, That the right hand of the Most High has changed.”
verses.11: I shall remember the deeds of the LORD; I will certainly remember Your wonders of old.
verses.12: I will meditate on all Your work, And on Your deeds with thanksgiving.
verses.13: Your way, God, is holy; What god is great like our God?
verses.14: You are the God who works wonders; You have made known Your strength among the peoples.
verses.15: By Your power You have redeemed Your people, The sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
verses.16: The waters saw You, God; The waters saw You, they were in anguish; The ocean depths also trembled.
verses.17: The clouds poured out water; The skies sounded out; Your arrows flashed here and there.
verses.18: The sound of Your thunder was in the whirlwind; The lightning lit up the world; The earth trembled and shook.
verses.19: Your way was in the sea And Your paths in the mighty waters, And Your footprints were not known.
verses.20: You led Your people like a flock By the hand of Moses and Aaron.
